title: 8 Herbal Teas For Swollen Gums
intro_desc: During pregnancy, many women experience swollen gums, also known as gingivitis. This is because hormonal changes cause their gums to become more sensitive and prone to inflammation. Swollen gums can be painful and make eating, speaking, and even smiling uncomfortable. They can also increase the risk of gum disease, which can lead to more serious health problems. For pregnant women, swollen gums can be especially challenging because they may need to eat nutritious foods to support their growing baby, but pain and discomfort can make mealtime a struggle.  Additionally, swollen gums can be a sign of an underlying issue that needs to be addressed, so it's essential for pregnant women to talk to their healthcare provider about their symptoms. In some cases, swollen gums can be a precursor to more severe pregnancy complications, such as preterm labor or low birth weight.  To help manage swollen gums, pregnant women can try using herbal teas that have anti-inflammatory properties. These teas can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. They are also a gentle, natural way to promote healthy gums and prevent further inflammation. When consumed regularly, these teas can help pregnant women maintain good oral health and reduce their risk of more serious complications.
faq_1: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe swollen gums. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce gum inflammation. They can also help calm the mouth and reduce pain. Regularly drinking herbal tea may help prevent swollen gums from forming, but this can vary from person to person.
faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea, especially peppermint and chamomile, may help ease swollen gums. These teas have anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and soothe the gums. The soothing effects of herbal tea can help calm irritated gums, making it a popular home remedy for swollen gums.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for swollen gums daily can be safe if you choose the right ones. Look for teas that have anti-inflammatory properties, like peppermint, sage, or echinacea. These can help reduce swelling and ease pain. However, be cautious with strong or bitter teas, as they might irritate your gums further.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help reduce swollen gums, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll see results. It can take anywhere from a few days to a week or two for your gums to start feeling better. Some people notice a difference sooner, while others may need a bit more time to see improvements.
faq_time: To help with swollen gums, drink herbal tea in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the active ingredients to stay in your system throughout the day, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Drinking too close to bedtime may interfere with a good night's sleep, making it harder for your gums to heal.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas, like ginkgo and St. John's Wort, can interact with prescription medications. This is especially true for blood thinners and medications for swollen gums. The interaction can cause bleeding or worsening of gum symptoms. If you take prescription medications, it's essential to be cautious when trying herbal teas to avoid any potential complications.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have swollen gums, you might be tempted to try herbal teas for relief. However, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas can be too strong or interact with other pregnancy-related changes, which can affect your baby's growth. It's generally recommended to stick with plain warm water or weak tea.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to calm swollen gums, but it's essential to be cautious when breastfeeding. Some herbs, like chamomile and peppermint, are generally considered safe, but others might not be suitable. As a precaution, research the specific herb before consumption to ensure it's free from any substances that could harm your baby.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas contains properties that may help with swollen gums. It has anti-inflammatory compounds like cichoric acid, caftaric acid, and chicoric acid that reduce swelling and inflammation. The tea also has antimicrobial properties from constituents like alkylamides and sesquiterpenes, which fight off bacteria and other pathogens that can cause gum infections.  If you have swollen gums, you can try making a tea by steeping dried Echinacea purpurea roots in hot water. Steep the roots for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 1-2 cups a day. You can also add honey to sweeten the tea if needed. To get the most out of the tea, try to take it at the same time every day, ideally in the morning and before bedtime. It may take a few days to notice any improvements in your gum health. From taking Echinacea purpurea tea, you may notice a decrease in swelling and redness of your gums. You may also experience a reduction in pain and discomfort associated with gum infections. In some cases, the tea may even help to prevent the formation of new infections and promote healing of existing ones. However, it's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea tea is not a replacement for medical treatment. If you have severe gum infections or other oral health issues, you should consult a dentist or healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas contains compounds like eucalyptol, which is known for its anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation. The tea also has antimicrobial properties, thanks to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help combat bacterial infections that can lead to gum problems. When using Eucalyptus globulus teas for swollen gums, it's recommended to brew a strong cup of tea, steeping 1-2 teaspoons of dried leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. The liquid should then be strained and cooled to a comfortable temperature before being used as a mouthwash or gargle, 2-3 times a day. As you use the tea, you can expect a reduction in swelling and pain, along with a decrease in the presence of plaque and bacteria in the gums. This can lead to improved gum health, a reduction in bleeding, and a fresher breath.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis teas contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, flavonoids, and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria that can cause swollen gums. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of Salvia officinalis can help soothe and calm the swollen gums, making it a popular natural remedy for oral health issues.  To use Salvia officinalis teas for swollen gums, you can make a tea by steeping 1 teaspoon of dried Salvia leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool before using it as a mouthwash. You can also add a pinch of salt to the tea to help reduce swelling and kill bacteria.  When you take action by drinking the Salvia officinalis tea or using it as a mouthwash, you can expect the inflammation to reduce, the pain to subside, and the swelling to go down. The antibacterial properties of the tea can also help prevent future infections and promote healthy gum growth. Regular use of Salvia officinalis tea may also help prevent gum recession and promote overall oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale teas contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the gums. These compounds work by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation. Ginger also has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections and swelling. To use Zingiber officinale teas for swollen gums, steep a teaspoon of dried ginger in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. You can drink this tea 2-3 times a day, or apply a cooled tea bag to the affected area for 5-10 minutes to reduce swelling and ease pain. After taking action, you can expect to see a reduction in swelling and inflammation, as well as a decrease in pain and discomfort. You may also notice an improvement in your gum health, with fewer signs of infection and inflammation. It's essential to note that while Zingiber officinale teas can provide relief from swollen gums, they should not replace regular dental care and professional treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis teas contains compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and limonene that have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and calm swollen gums. These properties can reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum inflammation. The tea is also rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant effects that can protect the gums from further damage and promote healing.  If you have swollen gums, consider drinking Melissa officinalis tea 2-3 times a day, after meals and before bedtime. You can steep 1 teaspoon of dried Melissa officinalis leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. You can also add a pinch of salt and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice to enhance its antiseptic properties.  As you take action, you may notice a reduction in swelling and pain within 2-3 days. The inflammation may subside, and the gums may appear healthier and more vibrant. The antiseptic properties of Melissa officinalis may help prevent infections and promote a healthy balance of oral bacteria. However, if your swollen gums persist or worsen, consult a dentist or healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium teas contains compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids that may help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections in the gums, which are common causes of swollen gums. The tea's antimicrobial properties can also help combat the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. To try Achillea millefolium tea for swollen gums, steep 1 teaspoon of dried herb in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day. Before taking action, it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions or are taking medications. If you're directed to try the tea, monitor your symptoms closely, and you may notice a decrease in gum swelling and inflammation within a few days. You may also experience relief from pain and discomfort. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, seek medical attention to rule out any underlying conditions that may require further treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Achillea millefolium herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums. The anti-inflammatory properties of glycyrrhizin, in particular, are effective in soothing swollen gums and gums pain.  If you have swollen gums, try drinking 1-2 cups of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea per day, preferably after meals. You can also add a teaspoon of glycerin to the tea for better results. To make the tea, steep 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool before drinking. Taking action may lead to a decrease in gum inflammation and swelling, making it easier to eat and talk. The flavonoids in the tea may also help to reduce the risk of gum disease and other oral health issues. However, it's essential to note that Glycyrrhiza glabra tea should not be used as a replacement for regular dental care. Consult your dentist before using the tea to treat swollen gums.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal teas for swollen gums

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas contains properties like flavonoids and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and fight off infections, making it a potential remedy for swollen gums. These properties have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help calm down swollen gums and prevent further irritation.  To try Ginkgo biloba teas for swollen gums, start by steeping one teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba leaves in a cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea and let it cool down before using it as a mouthwash or gargling it to help soothe your gums. You can also drink the tea as a warm beverage, but be sure to rinse your mouth with water afterwards to remove any remaining tea residue. When taking Ginkgo biloba teas for swollen gums, you can likely experience a reduction in swelling and inflammation within a few days. Your gums may start to feel less tender and painful, and you may notice a decrease in bleeding or discharge. However, if your symptoms persist or worsen, it's essential to consult with your dentist or healthcare provider to rule out any underlying conditions that may require medical attention.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for swollen gums
